name: Formula 1
description: F1 drivers, circuits, teams, and racing history

questions.q: In what year was the first Formula 1 World Championship held?

source.notes: First race held at Silverstone, England. Giuseppe Farina won the inaugural championship.

questions.q: Where was the first Formula 1 race held?

source.notes: May 13, 1950. Still hosts the British Grand Prix today.

questions.q: What famous prancing animal is featured on Ferrari's logo?

source.notes: 'Cavallino Rampante' (prancing horse). Adopted from WWI flying ace Francesco Baracca.

questions.q: What does the 'DRS' acronym stand for in modern Formula 1?

source.notes: Opens the rear wing to reduce drag, increasing speed. Introduced in 2011.

questions.q: Formula 1 champion Kimi Räikkönen hails from which country?

source.notes: Known as 'The Iceman.' 2007 World Champion with Ferrari.

questions.q: What drink do F1 winners traditionally spray from the podium?

source.notes: Tradition began in 1966 at Le Mans with Dan Gurney. Today usually Moët & Chandon or Ferrari Trento.

questions.q: What does the yellow-and-red striped flag indicate to drivers?

source.original_a: Warns drivers of a slippery track
source.notes: Usually indicates oil, water, or debris on track.

questions.q: Which city hosted the first F1 night race in 2008?

source.notes: Marina Bay Street Circuit. Held under floodlights because of TV-friendly European times.

questions.q: Which driver has won the most Formula 1 World Championships (7, tied with Michael Schumacher)?

source.notes: Tied with Schumacher at 7 titles. First Black driver to win the championship.

questions.q: In what year did Michael Schumacher first join Ferrari?

source.notes: Joined from Benetton. Won 5 consecutive titles with Ferrari (2000-2004).

questions.q: The 'Tifosi' are the passionate fans of which F1 team?

source.notes: Italian for 'supporters.' Named after the red 'rosso corsa' livery of Ferrari.

questions.q: Which F1 team is based in Brackley, England, and dominated the 2014-2020 era?

source.notes: Mercedes won 8 consecutive constructors' titles (2014-2021).

questions.q: The Monaco Grand Prix is held on the streets of which city?

source.notes: Iconic street circuit through Monte Carlo, Monaco. First F1 race held in 1950.

questions.q: Who was the youngest driver to win the F1 World Championship?

source.notes: Vettel won his first title at age 23 in 2010 with Red Bull.

questions.q: How many drivers are on each F1 team's roster for a race?

source.notes: Two 'race drivers.' Teams can also have reserve/test drivers.

questions.q: Max Verstappen drives for which country on the international stage?

source.notes: Born in Hasselt, Belgium, but races under the Dutch flag. Father Jos raced for Netherlands too.

questions.q: What is the name of the famous 'Eau Rouge' corner located at which circuit?

source.notes: In Belgium. One of the most famous corners in F1. Named after a local river.

questions.q: Who died in a tragic crash at the San Marino Grand Prix in 1994, considered one of the greatest F1 drivers of all time?

source.notes: May 1, 1994 at Imola. Three-time World Champion. Brazilian legend.

questions.q: Which F1 team's drivers have included Alain Prost, Ayrton Senna, and Mika Häkkinen?

source.notes: McLaren. Also home to Lewis Hamilton's first championship (2008).

questions.q: What color are the current Ferrari F1 cars?

source.notes: 'Rosso Corsa' (racing red). Traditional Italian racing color since the 1920s.
